title: [never-merge] windows,add: allow creating windows friendly layer by adding `PAX` headers.
body:
Add windows friendly pax headers to layers when building for windows

Example
```dockerfile
FROM mcr.microsoft.com/windows/nanoserver:ltsc2022
ADD emptyfile .
```

Never Merge this PR, since buildah CI is not capable enough to test
this.

Closes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/4010

We have no way to test this hence its decided not to add this patch to buildah. This PR is just here for dicussion and will close it since **buildah CI is not capable enough to test this**. 


Test this manually by inspect layers and most likely layers must contain header

Testfile
```dockerfile
FROM mcr.microsoft.com/windows/nanoserver:ltsc2022
ADD emptyfile .
```

Would this actually work in respection mixed case files/directories.  

IE If I copied in a FOOBAR.txt on /foobar.txt and the file foobar.TXT existed in the container, would it update the foobar.TXT file, or create a new file with the mixed case name?
@flouthoc Lets talk about this at standup.

I've tried this out, and on a Linux build system, I was able to build a Windows container image and run it on a Windows server using containerd.

I understand there are CI limitations, but it would be great if this work could make it in, with the proper detection for Windows/Linux container images.

> I've tried this out, and on a Linux build system, I was able to build a Windows container image and run it on a Windows server using containerd.
> 
> I understand there are CI limitations, but it would be great if this work could make it in, with the proper detection for Windows/Linux container images.

Hi @sebsoto Thanks for trying and verifying this patch :smile: . I'll try to prepare a test for this but from discussion with maintainers we were unable to decide priority on this one. 

Could you let us know who is using this feature and what would be the priority. 

Could you respond to this and continue discussion on original issue so context stays there https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/4010 :)

Thanks
